title: helianthus laciniatus subsp. crenatus
intro: Helianthus laciniatis subsp. crenatus is a lesser-known species of sunflower native to Europe, characterized by its unique, toothed leaves and smaller flower heads compared to other sunflower species. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Helianthus laciniatis subsp. crenatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile addition to traditional remedies and modern herbalism practices.
